architectureNotes: Edit Metadata is not a dropped widget: the TOC per-step kebab menu (.tocMenuDiv → .tocPageIconMenu) item 'Edit Metadata' (li ng-click="tocSectionEditPopup") re-opens the step creation wizard (#formsModal / .forms-modal) in editingStepData mode.
The kebab menu exposes Clone New Level, Edit Metadata and Delete (Delete gated by canDeletePage) — QC TC_32.

Editable wizard fields: Step Title (#title, ng-model="stepTitle", required), Keyword Tags (#tag / #tag-list, the 'standards/objectives' tags), Levels of Support (#level-sec; new step LoS fixed to 'Core', read-only), Instructions for Students/Teachers (#studentDesc/#teacherDesc, 10000-char budget).

There is NO literal 'Objectives' or 'Standards' field in the metadata wizard markup; those QC concepts are mapped to Keyword Tags + Levels of Support + Instructions, which are the actual fields present.
Save = #subBtn ng-click="... infoEdit()" (visible only off the Basic Info tab); Cancel = #cancelBtn ng-click="cancelAndReset()". 'Change Step' (#backPageTray) is hidden while editing.
Required-field validation is enforced via ng-class disabledBtn / !stepTitle on Next/Submit rather than an inline red error string, so the spec asserts the disabled-button contract.

RTE toolbar formatting (B/I/U, lists, image, undo/redo, char-limit toast), Publish/Republish/Reader visibility, read-only permission mode, responsive layout, simultaneous-tab conflict, clone/delete mutations and persistence-after-reload are outside the authoring DOM and are deferred.
